task_completion.description: Verify the CT Cranium DICOM dataset is loaded into the application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius GUI should display the loaded CT scan, showing axial, sagittal, and coronal 2D slice views of a human skull.

task_completion.description: Verify a mask is created using the 'Bone' preset (226-3071 HU).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The masking/thresholding panel should show the 'Bone' preset selected, or the threshold values explicitly set to Min: 226 and Max: 3071. The 2D views should show the bone structures highlighted by the mask.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'raw' 3D surface model is generated from the mask.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer panel should display a 3D model of the skull that includes the main cranium as well as smaller, disconnected floating bone fragments or noise.

task_completion.description: Verify the raw 3D surface is exported as a binary STL to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or 'Export' dialog box should be visible, saving the file as 'bone_raw.stl' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory. The format should be set to STL (Binary).

task_completion.description: Verify the surface is cleaned to contain ONLY the largest region (the main skull).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GUI should show the use of surface selection/editing tools (like 'Select Region' or 'Surface properties') to isolate the largest part. The 3D viewer must update to show ONLY the main skull, with all smaller floating fragments removed.

task_completion.description: Verify the cleaned 3D surface is exported as a binary STL to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or 'Export' dialog box should be visible, saving the newly cleaned surface as 'bone_cleaned.stl' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory. The format should be set to STL (Binary).

integrity.description: The agent must use the InVesalius GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through the InVesalius menus, thresholding panels, and 3D surface generation tools, rather than executing a script in the terminal to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent must actually perform a cleaning/isolation step between the two exports.
integrity.visual_evidence: There must be visual evidence of the 3D model changing (floating fragments disappearing) or surface selection tools being used between the saving of 'bone_raw.stl' and 'bone_cleaned.stl'. The agent should not just save the exact same model twice under different names.

integrity.description: The 3D surfaces must be genuinely generated and rendered in the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer pane in InVesalius must display the rendered skull models before they are exported. The agent should not just create empty files or use a text editor to spoof the STL files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses InVesalius v3.0. The dataset is a real CT Cranium scan (108 slices). The 'Bone' preset corresponds exactly to a threshold range of 226-3071 Hounsfield Units (HU). The 'raw' surface generated from this mask will naturally contain the main skull plus smaller disconnected bone fragments/noise. The 'cleaned' surface requires the agent to use InVesalius's surface tools to isolate the largest region (the main skull) and discard the smaller fragments. Both files must be saved as binary STLs in /home/ga/Documents/.
